title: Powered seat and related seating improvements
abstract: A powered seat (1) for assisting a person in moving between seated and standing positions, the powered seat (1) comprising a seat squab having front and rear movable portions (2a, 2b,) and a mechanism enabling movement of the seat squab portions (2a, 2b) between a seated configuration in which the portions (2a, 2b) are substantially aligned for providing a seat, and a standing configuration in which the rear seat squab portion (2b) is higher than the front seat squab portion (2a) and both seat squab portions (2a, 2b) are tilted forwards at different angles.
claims:
Claims 1. A powered seat for assisting a person in moving between seated and standing positions, the powered seat comprising a seat squab having front and rear movable portions, and a mechanism enabling movement of the seat squab portions between a seated configuration in which the portions are substantially aligned for providing a seat, and a standing configuration in which the rear seat squab portion is higher than the front seat squab portion and both seat squab portions are tilted forwards at different angles. 2. A powered seat as claimed in claim 1, in which the seat squab portions are substantially adjacent in the seated configuration, and substantially spaced apart in the standing configuration. 3. A powered seat as claimed in claim 1 or 2, in which the rear seat squab portion is tiltable independently of the front seat squab portion. 4. A powered seat as claimed in any of claims 1 to 3, in which the seat squab portions have complementary shapes for fitting together in the seated configuration. 5. A powered seat as claimed in claim 4, in which the complementary shape of one seat squab portion is a projection with a flexible front, and the front is tethered to part of the seat for downward deformation in the standing configuration. 6. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which the rear seat squab portion lies in a region over the front seat squab portion in the standing configuration. 7. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which a back rest is provided adjacent to the seat squab, the back rest being movable with one of the seat squab portions when the seat is transitioned between seated and standing configurations. 8. A powered seat as claimed in claim 7, in which the back rest is substantially upright in the standing configuration. 9. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which at least one grip portion is provided adjacent to the seat squab, the grip portions(s) being movable with the rear seat squab portion when the seat is transitioned between seated and standing configurations. 10. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which a footrest is provided in a region below the seat squab, and in the seated configuration the footrest is movable between stowed and deployed positions. 11. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which a control is provided for operating the mechanism. 12. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, when dependent on claim 7, in which the back rest and the rear seat squab portion are moveable as a unit during operation of the mechanism. 13. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, when dependent on claim 9, in which the grip portion(s) and the rear seat squab portion are moveable as a unit during operation of the mechanism. 14. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, when dependent on claims 7 and 9, in which the back rest, the grip portion(s) and the rear seat squab portion are moveable as a unit during operation of the mechanism. 15. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which the mechanism for moving the seat squab portions includes a moveable platform connected to the rear seat squab portion, for causing the rear seat squab portion to separate from or converge with the front seat squab portion during operation of the mechanism. 16. A powered seat as claimed in claim 15, in which, when the seat squab portions are separated during use, the mechanism is adapted to move the platform without substantially changing its tilt. 17. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, in which the mechanism is a powered lift mechanism and the seat includes a supporting structure, the lift mechanism comprising a first support for the seat squab, the first support having a pivotal connection to a front region of the supporting structure for changing the tilt angle of the first support; a second support for the rear seat squab portion, the second support being pivotally connected to a rear region of the first support, for changing the tilt angle of the second support relative to the first support, and moveable to a region higher than the first support; an actuator adapted to move the first and second supports; a control for operating the actuator; and a lifter engageable with the second support above a predetermined tilt angle of the first support, and adapted to pivot the second support relative to the first support, for in use separating or bringing together the seat squab portions. 18. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a cover for covering the mechanism, the cover comprising a flexible sheet connected at a first end to a back of the powered seat, and connected at a second end to one or more points below the first end, support means for holding the sheet substantially taut, and sheet distribution means adapted to redistribute the flexible sheet during operation of the mechanism, for maintaining substantial coverage of the mechanism irrespective of the position of the mechanism. 19. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a base, an axle and at least one wheel mounted about the axle, and a slot provided through the base, the axle being located in the slot, the slot including a first axle receiving area where the axle is loosely retained, and a second axle receiving area where the axle is restrained against 

 upward movement and the at least one wheel supports the weight of the powered seat, the weight of the powered seat maintaining engagement of the axle in the second receiving area. 20. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, further comprising a retractable footrest, the footrest including deployment means for moving the footrest, and a mechanical linkage comprising first and second links of substantially equal length, which are pivotally connected to the footrest by joints at different positions, a third link pivotally connected to each of the first and second links by joints, to connect the first and second links together, and a fourth link pivotally connected to each of the first and second links by joints, to connect the first and second links together, the fourth link being parallel to the third link and displaced from the third link along the first and second links, each of the third and fourth links crossing the first link and connecting to the second link at one end, and being adapted for connection to the seat at another end, and the extent of each of the third and fourth links between the first and second links being substantially the same, enabling the first and second links to remain substantially parallel in operation. 21. A powered seat as claimed in any preceding claim, as part of a chair. 22. A powered lift mechanism for a seat squab of a chair, the seat squab including front and rear portions, and the chair including a supporting structure, the lift mechanism comprising a first support for the seat squab, the first support having a pivotal connection to a front region of the supporting structure for changing the tilt angle of the first support; a second support for the rear seat squab portion, the second support being pivotally connected to a rear region of the first support, for changing the tilt angle of the second support relative to the first support, and moveable to a region higher than the first support; 

 an actuator adapted to move the first and second supports; a control for operating the actuator; and a lifter engageable with the second support above a predetermined tilt angle of the first support, and adapted to pivot the second support relative to the first support, for in use separating or bringing together the seat squab portions. 23. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in claim 22, in which the second support is disposed at an angle of substantially between 70Â° and 85Â° to the vertical when engaged with the lifter. 24. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in claim 22 or 23, in which the first support is tiltable to an angle substantially between 15Â° and 45Â° from the vertical. 25. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in any of claims 22 to 24, in which the first and second supports are moveable upward and tiltable forward by extension of the actuator. 26. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in any of claims 22 to 25, in which the lifter is pivotally connected to a rear region of the support about an axis parallel to the pivotal connection of the support. 27. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in any of claims 22 to 26, in which the lifter is adapted to engage and pivot the second support in an opposite direction to a pivotal direction of the first support. 28. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in any of claims 22 to 27, as part of a chair having a seat squab with front and rear portions. 29. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in claim 28, in which the chair further includes a back rest with a reclining mechanism for moving the back rest between reclined and upright positions, and the control is adapted to use the reclining mechanism to bring the back rest substantially upright prior to moving the seat squab portions. 30. A powered lift mechanism as claimed in claim 28 or 29, in which the chair further includes a retractable footrest with a deployment mechanism for moving the footrest between stowed and deployed positions, and the control is adapted to use the deployment mechanism to stow the footrest prior to moving the seat squab portions. 31. A chair comprising a seat squab lifting mechanism, and a cover for covering the lifting mechanism, the cover comprising a flexible sheet connected at a first end to a back of the chair, and connected at a second end to one or more points below the first end, support means for holding the sheet substantially taut, and sheet distribution means adapted to redistribute the flexible sheet during operation of the lifting mechanism, for maintaining substantial coverage of the lifting mechanism irrespective of the position of the lifting mechanism. 32. A chair as claimed in claim 31, further comprising at least two bars arranged across the back of the chair, the bars being provided substantially parallel to each other and to the ends of the flexible sheet, and the flexible sheet passing around an underside of one bar and a top of the other bar for forming a pulley system. 33. A chair as claimed in claim 31 or 32, in which a resilient sprung connection links the second end of the cover to the one or more points. 34. A chair as claimed in claim 32, or claim 33 when dependent on claim 32, further comprising a third bar connected to the upper bar about the flexible sheet, the third bar including a resilient sprung connection for connection to a point above the upper and third bars. 35. A chair as claimed in any of claims 32 to 34, in which one or more of the bars each include a roller, each roller extending along and being rotatable about the respective bar. 36. A wheeled chair comprising a base, an axle and at least one wheel mounted about the axle, and a slot provided through the base, the axle being located in the slot, the slot including a first axle receiving area where the axle is loosely retained, and a second axle receiving area where the axle is restrained against upward movement and the at least one wheel supports the weight of the chair, the weight of the chair maintaining engagement of the axle in the second receiving area. 37. A wheeled chair as claimed in claim 36, in which the axle is movable between the axle receiving areas by tilting the base about an axis parallel to and displaced from the axle. 38. A wheeled chair as claimed in claim 36 or 37, in which the axle receiving areas are at different ends of the slot, and the first axle receiving area is disposed higher than the second axle receiving area. 39. A wheeled chair as claimed in any of claims 36 to 38, in which there are two slots of the same shape and provided at the same relative position on opposite sides of the base. 40. A wheeled chair as claimed in any of claims 36 to 39, in which the or each slot has a substantially U-shaped, V-shaped or J-shaped cross-section. 41. A wheeled chair as claimed in any of claims 36 to 40, in which the at least one wheel is located inside the base for concealment. 42. A retractable footrest for a chair, the footrest comprising deployment means for moving the footrest, and a mechanical linkage comprising first and second links of substantially equal length, which are pivotally connected to the footrest by joints at different positions, a third link pivotally connected to each of the first and second links by joints, to connect the first and second links together, and 

 a fourth link pivotally connected to each of the first and second links by joints, to connect the first and second links together, the fourth link being parallel to the third link and displaced from the third link along the first and second links, each of the third and fourth links crossing the first link and connecting to the second link at one end, and being adapted for connection to the chair at another end, and the extent of each of the third and fourth links between the first and second links being substantially the same, enabling the first and second links to remain substantially parallel in operation. 43. A retractable footrest as claimed in claim 42, in which the deployment means includes an actuator arranged to deploy the footrest when extended, and to retract the footrest when contracted. 44. A retractable footrest as claimed in claim 43, including a control connected to the actuator for use in deploying and retracting the footrest. 45. A retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 44, in which the deployment mechanism includes two mechanical linkages, each connected to the footrest. 46. A retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 45, in which one or both of the first and second links are non-linear. 47. A retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 46, in which one or both of the first and second links include a bend at the respective joints where they connect to the fourth link. 48. A retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 47, in which one or both of the third and fourth links are non-linear. 49. A retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 48, in which one or both of the third and fourth links include a bend at the respective joints where they connect to the first link. 50. A retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 49, in which the ends of the third and fourth links are pivotally connected at different positions. 51. A chair comprising a retractable footrest as claimed in any of claims 42 to 50. 52. A chair comprising one or more of: a powered seat as claimed in any of claims 1 to 21; a powered lift mechanism according to any of claims 22 to 30, and optionally a cover according to any of claims 31 to 35; a wheeled chair according to any of claims 36 to 41; a retractable footrest according to any of claims 42 to 51.
